requirements_met: Created deploy/nas/docker-compose.yml with pgvector:pg16 and NAS-specific configuration
Created deploy/nas/initdb/01_extensions.sql with vector extension initialization
Created deploy/nas/deploy.sh as idempotent deployment script with GATE G2
Created deploy/nas/smoke.sh with validation gates G3-G5
Created deploy/nas/.env.deploy.example with five required fields and password generation comment
Both shell scripts pass bash -n syntax validation
validation_results.test_output_summary: 18 tests passed in 0.06s (regression check for infrastructure changes)
validation_results.code_quality: Excellent - scripts follow best practices with set -euo pipefail, proper error handling, explicit gate labeling, secure credential handling (no plaintext passwords, SSH key auth only), and comprehensive comments documenting firewall expectations
edge_cases_covered: deploy.sh exits non-zero on container startup failure (GATE G2)
smoke.sh validates pg_isready, pgvector extension presence, direct network connectivity, and PG_VERSION file
smoke.sh exits code 1 on any gate failure for fail-fast CI integration
.env.deploy covered by existing .gitignore to prevent credential leaks

criteria_verification.notes: deploy/nas/docker-compose.yml contains all required elements: pgvector/pgvector:pg16 image, fleet_memory_postgres service, restart: unless-stopped, ${NAS_DOCKER_ROOT}/pgdata bind mount, 5432:5432 port mapping, env_file: .env, initdb volume mount, and comprehensive firewall documentation specifying LAN + Tailscale CGNAT range 100.64.0.0/10
deploy/nas/initdb/01_extensions.sql contains CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector; statement, matching deploy/local pattern with appropriate NAS-specific comment

deploy/nas/deploy.sh is executable, has set -euo pipefail, sources .env.deploy with validation, performs rsync + remote .env render + docker compose up -d per runbook Phases 2-3, implements GATE G2 with PASS/FAIL labels, uses SSH key auth only (no sshpass, no plaintext passwords), exits non-zero on failure

deploy/nas/smoke.sh is executable with set -euo pipefail, implements GATE G3 (pg_isready + pgvector extension check via SSH docker exec), GATE G4 (psql direct network connection over LAN/Tailscale DSN), GATE G5 (PG_VERSION=16 file check at ${NAS_DOCKER_ROOT}/pgdata), all gates have explicit PASS/FAIL labels, exits code 1 on any gate failure

deploy/nas/.env.deploy.example committed with all five required fields (NAS_HOST, NAS_USER, NAS_SSH_PORT, NAS_DOCKER_ROOT, FLEET_MEMORY_PG_PASSWORD) as empty values with descriptive comments, includes openssl rand -base64 24 generation comment. Verified .gitignore line 151 contains .env rule covering .env.deploy via .env* wildcard pattern

bash -n deploy/nas/deploy.sh && bash -n deploy/nas/smoke.sh exited 0 with 'Syntax check PASSED' output. Shellcheck attempted but not available on system (optional per AC). Both scripts pass bash syntax validation

Created files are shell scripts (.sh), YAML (docker-compose.yml), SQL (.sql), and environment template (.env.deploy.example) - none are Python files subject to project's ruff linting. Shell scripts passed bash -n syntax validation (AC-006), YAML structure is valid docker-compose format, SQL syntax is standard PostgreSQL DDL. No linting errors for the file types created

rationale: All 7 acceptance criteria verified against deterministic evidence bundle. Implementation complete with all required deployment artifacts (docker-compose.yml, deploy.sh, smoke.sh, .env.deploy.example, initdb/01_extensions.sql) correctly structured. Scripts follow best practices (set -euo pipefail, proper error handling), implement all required gates (G2-G5) with explicit PASS/FAIL labels, meet security requirements (SSH key auth only, no plaintext credentials), and pass syntax validation. Independent test verification confirms no regression (18 existing tests passed). Honesty verification perfect (1.0 score, zero discrepancies). All quality gates passed. One advisory warning about missing Phase 3 specialist invocation is non-blocking per evidence bundle categorization.
